expressions.korean: 빨리 먹다
expressions.english: to eat fast
expressions.notes: 먹다 = to eat; to drink

expressions.korean: 말이 빠르다
expressions.english: to have a fast way of talking; to speak fast
expressions.notes: 말 = language; what one says; expression; word; term

expressions.korean: 눈치가 빠르다
expressions.english:
to have a quick sense of what is happening
to have a fast intuition
expressions.notes: 눈치 = intuition; quick sense; perceptiveness

expressions.korean: 걸음이 빠르다
expressions.english:
to have a fast walking pace
to walk fast
expressions.notes: 걸음 = step; (walking) pace

expressions.korean: 시간이 너무 빨라요.
expressions.english: Time goes too fast.
expressions.notes:
시간 = time; hour
너무 = too much, excessively; very

expressions.korean: 제 말이 너무 빠르나요?
expressions.english:
Are my words too fast?
Am I speaking too fast?
expressions.notes: 제 = (polite) my; I

expressions.korean: 왜 그렇게 밥을 빨리 먹어요?
expressions.english: Why are you eating your meal so quickly?
expressions.notes:
왜 = why; for what reason; how come
그렇게 = like that; such + adjective/adverb
밥 = food; meal; cooked rice

expressions.korean: 친구들이 제 걸음이 빠르대요.
expressions.english:
My friends tell me I have a fast (walking) pace.
My friends tell me I walk fast.
expressions.notes:
친구 = friend
-들 = is a suffix used to indicate plural.
-데요 = I heard that + S + V. They say + S + V

expressions.korean: 저 곧 가야 되니까 빨리 주세요.
expressions.english: I have to go soon, so hurry and give it (to me).
expressions.notes:
저 = (polite) I; me
곧 = soon
가다 = to go; to leave
-이/어/여 되다 = to have to + V
-(으)니까 = since, because
주다 = to give
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative

expressions.korean: 우리 이거 빨리 끝내고 놀러 가요.
expressions.english: Let's finish this quickly and go hang out.
expressions.notes:
우리 = we; us
이거 = this thing; this
끝내다 = to finish; to get + N + done
놀러 가다 = to go on a trip; to hang out; to visit
-이/어/여요 = imperative; Let's + V

expressions.korean: 빠르다고 다 좋은 것은 아니에요.
expressions.english: Just because it is fast doesn't mean it's good.
expressions.notes:
-다고 = just because; saying that S + V
다 = all; every (thing)
좋다 = to be good, likable; to be desirable; to be nice; to like
아니다 = to be not

expressions.korean: 여기 인터넷 속도 진짜 빨르네요.
expressions.english: The speed of the Internet here is really fast.
expressions.notes:
여기 = here, this place
인터넷 = the Internet
속도 = speed; pace
-(으)네요 = is a sentence ending that expresses realization of a fact or agreement to a statement.

expressions.korean: 빨리 끝내 주시면 좋을 것 같아요.
expressions.english: It would be good if you get it done quickly.
expressions.notes:
-이/어/여 주다 = to do something for someone
-(으)면 = if + S + V; when/once + S + V
-(으) 것 같다 = I think + S + will/be going to; it seems like + S + will/be going to

expressions.korean: 어느 길로 가는 게 제일 빠를까요?
expressions.english: Which way will be the fastest?
expressions.notes:
어느 = which; some
길 = road, street; way
제일 = the best; the most

expressions.korean: 저 지금 시간 없으니까 빨리 말해요.
expressions.english: I don't have time right now, so talk fast.
expressions.notes:
지금 = now
없다 = to not have; to not be there; to not exist
말하다 = to talk; to say; to speak; to tell

expressions.korean: 이 책을 보면 이해가 빨리 될 거예요.
expressions.english: If you read this book, you will quickly understand.
expressions.notes:
이 = this
책 = book
보다 = to see; to look; to watch; to meet up; to read
책을 보다 = to look at a book; to read a book
이해 = understanding
이해가 되다 = can understand; something makes sense
-(으)ㄹ 것이다 = will; to be going to + V

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 말이 빨라서 알아듣기 힘들 어요.
expressions.english: That person speaks so fast that it's hard to understand.
expressions.notes:
그 사람 = that person; he, she
알아듣다 = to understand what one says
-기 힘들다 = to be difficult/hard to + V

expressions.korean: 제일 빨리 가려면 지하철을 타야 될 것 같아요.
expressions.english: To get there quickly, I think we should take the subway.
expressions.notes:
-(으)려면 = in order to + V
지하철 = subway
타다 = to ride; to get on; to take (vehicle)

expressions.korean: 그런 일이 있었으면 빨리 자한테 이야 기를 했었어야죠.
expressions.english: If there was an incident like that, you should have told me promptly.
expressions.notes:
그런 = (something) like that; such + noun
일 = work; task; thing, stuff; occasion
있다 = to be there; to exist; to have
이야기 = story; what one says
이야기를 하다 = to talk, speak, say, tell; to have a conversation; to tell a story
-이/어/여/였어야 = you should have + p.p.

expressions.korean: 버스가 너무 빨리 달려서 버스에 서 있다가 넘어질 뻔 했어요.
expressions.english: Since the bus (I was riding) was going so fast, I nearly fell over while standing.
expressions.notes:
버스 = bus
달리다 = to run
-이/어/여서 = because, since
서 있다 = to be standing
-다가 = as a result of + V-ing
넘어지다 = to fall (down); to trip
-(으)ㄹ 뻔 하다 = to almost + V
